title: General Manager
description:
Core Accountabilities / Responsibilities:

  • Oversee ICF International, Singapore and China Project Operation
  • Monitor and manages activity plan for the Departments in line with the project schedule
  • Monitor and manages activity plan with Operation, Purchasing & Logistic, Design & Engineering, Production and Planning departments
  • Oversees day-to-day running of the operation activities based on the goals, objectives, and standards of the organization
  • Liaises with D&E and SCM on engineering and supply chain issues
  • Leads and manages the Project Team, including the overseeing of all day-to-day project activities
  • Leads internal and external project meetings to ensure alignment
  • Assists in identifying internal and external issues, including their expectations
  • Assists in identifying the risks and opportunities, and actions to address such risks and opportunities
  • Monitors the Departments performance to ensure objectives are met
  • Ensures Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) regulations are adhered by all employees who are involved in the operation activities
  • Reports to COO TOP Management on regular basis regarding operation and all the Project status

Requirements:

Education & Certification:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Maritime Studies, maritime operations or a related field.

Experience:

  • Seafaring Chief Engineer (COC1) with at least 20 years related managerial skills with minimum 5 years of experience in ship management or a related field.
  • Good stake-holders management skills, self-driven and resourceful team player with proven communication skills, able to mentor & train the team.
  • Experience in handling and managing alternative low carbon fueled ships such as LNG or Methanol fueled ships will be required.
  • Experience with dealing with Dual Fuel (DF) vessel management, new build vessel delivery or must have sailed as an Engineer on ships with DF propulsion systems.
  • In depth knowledge on dry docking in line with industry standards, including class, flag and other regulatory/statutory requirements.
  • Familiarity with ISM /ISPS/CSO and kept abreast of maritime developments.
  • Appointment will commensurate with experience.

Skills & Competencies:

  • Strong leadership and team management skills with the ability to oversee cross-functional project teams.
  • Deep technical expertise in maritime operations, including LNG/Methanol-fueled vessels and Dual Fuel propulsion systems.
  • Proficient in strategic planning, operational execution, and aligning departmental activities with project goals.
  • Experienced in managing stakeholders, conducting risk assessments, and ensuring compliance with safety and maritime regulations.
  • Skilled in project oversight, including new vessel delivery, dry docking, and regulatory adherence.
  • Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities, with a focus on continuous improvement and performance monitoring.
